title: [Falcon] Custom EQ Save Button — Persistence after Save EQ + Confirm dialog
steps: Precondition: Falcon device (Voyager Free 60+ UC) connected to Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom
Navigate away: Device → Device Info screen
Navigate back: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
expected: Expected: All 6 slider positions are restored exactly to +6, +3, 0, -3, -6, +3 dB respectively. Actual (If Bug): Slider positions reset to flat (0dB) or default values, even though preset name shows 'Custom'. Bug signature: Save EQ button + Confirm dialog fails to persist individual slider values to storage.

title: [Falcon] Custom EQ Save Button — Cancel dialog does NOT save
steps: Precondition: Falcon device connected. Current EQ preset is 'Flat'.
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom
Navigate away: Device → Device Info screen
Navigate back: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
Tap into EQ and verify all sliders are at 0dB (Flat default)
expected: Expected: EQ remains at 'Flat' preset with all sliders at 0dB. Actual (If Bug): EQ shows 'Custom' preset or sliders retain the modified values despite Cancel being tapped. Bug signature: Cancel dialog handler incorrectly triggers save, or dialog dismissal doesn't roll back the in-memory slider state.

title: [Falcon] Custom EQ Save Button — IMMEDIATE app kill after Confirm (no navigation away)
steps: Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom
IMMEDIATELY force-kill the app (swipe from recents) — do NOT navigate away first
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
expected: Expected: Slider positions persist at +3, +6, -3 dB after app force-kill and relaunch. Actual (If Bug): Slider positions reset to default (0dB) because Save EQ handler writes to in-memory state only and does not commit to persistent storage before app termination. Bug signature: Save EQ → Confirm does not synchronously persist to disk/firmware.

title: [Falcon] Custom EQ Save Button — Persistence across Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect
steps: Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom
Disconnect Bluetooth from phone Settings (or turn off Falcon)
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
expected: Expected: Slider positions persist at -6, +6, +3, -3 dB after BT disconnect/reconnect cycle. Actual (If Bug): Slider positions reset to flat (0dB) because Save EQ writes to app-local storage but fails to sync to device firmware, and device reconnection triggers a firmware state read that overwrites app state with defaults.

title: [Falcon] Custom EQ Save Button — Phone reboot persistence
steps: Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom
Verify EQ is applied (audio output reflects boosted bass and treble)
After reboot, open Poly Studio Mobile app
Wait for Falcon device to connect automatically
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
expected: Expected: Slider positions persist at +6, -3, +3, +6 dB after full phone reboot. Actual (If Bug): Slider positions reset to default (0dB) because Save EQ data is stored in app cache (not persistent storage) and is wiped on reboot. Bug signature: Save EQ does not write to SharedPreferences or database that survives app process restart + phone reboot.

title: [Falcon] Custom EQ Save Button — Rapid slider changes then Save
steps: Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom
Rapidly drag all 6 sliders to extreme values in sequence: 32Hz to +12dB, 125Hz to -12dB, 500Hz to +12dB, 1kHz to -12dB, 4kHz to +12dB, 8kHz to -12dB (complete within 3 seconds)
Navigate away: Device → Device Info screen
Navigate back: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom
expected: Expected: All 6 slider positions are saved exactly at extreme values (+12/-12 dB). Actual (If Bug): Some or all sliders are at different values (e.g., 0dB or partial values) because rapid slider changes caused race conditions in the Save EQ handler — the handler reads slider positions before all UI updates are committed, or the firmware sync command truncates values. Bug signature: Save EQ captures stale/intermediate slider state instead of final positions.

title: [Falcon] Custom EQ Save Button — Save, then modify without saving, then verify original save persists
steps: Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom
Navigate away and back to verify Profile A is saved (sliders at +6, -6, +6)
Without leaving the Custom EQ screen, modify sliders to: 32Hz = -6dB, 1kHz = +6dB, 8kHz = -6dB (Profile B)
Navigate away: Device → Device Info screen
Navigate back: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom
Verify slider positions are Profile A: 32Hz = +6dB, 1kHz = -6dB, 8kHz = +6dB
expected: Expected: Slider positions remain at Profile A (+6, -6, +6 dB) — the unsaved Profile B modifications are discarded. Actual (If Bug): Slider positions show Profile B (-6, +6, -6 dB) because the app auto-saves slider changes on navigation away, bypassing the Save EQ button + Confirm dialog flow. Bug signature: Save EQ button is rendered meaningless if navigation away also triggers a save.

title: [Falcon] Custom EQ Save Button — Background app during confirmation dialog
steps: Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom
Confirmation dialog appears — do NOT tap Confirm or Cancel yet
Press Home button to background the app
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app from recents
Observe the confirmation dialog is still visible
Navigate away: Device → Device Info screen
Navigate back: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom
expected: Expected: Slider positions persist at +9, -6, +9 dB after backgrounding during dialog and then confirming. Actual (If Bug): Slider positions reset to default (0dB) because the dialog's state was lost during backgrounding (Activity destroyed and recreated), and the Confirm callback references a stale or null slider state. Bug signature: Dialog survives backgrounding visually but its save callback loses reference to the slider values.

title: [Falcon] Custom EQ Save Button — Device asymmetry: Verify Falcon-specific EQ band count and range
steps: Precondition: Falcon device (Voyager Free 60+ UC) connected.
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom
Verify the number of EQ bands/sliders displayed matches Falcon specification (6 bands: 32Hz, 125Hz, 500Hz, 1kHz, 4kHz, 8kHz)
Verify each slider range is -12dB to +12dB (or as per Falcon spec)
Set each slider to its maximum value (+12dB)
Navigate away and back, verify all sliders at +12dB
Set each slider to its minimum value (-12dB)
Navigate away and back, verify all sliders at -12dB
expected: Expected: Falcon device shows exactly 6 bands with -12dB to +12dB range, and extreme values persist after Save EQ → Confirm → navigation. Actual (If Bug): Different number of bands (e.g., 5 or 10 bands) or different range (e.g., -6dB to +6dB) displayed on Falcon vs. other devices, or extreme values are clamped/truncated on save. Bug signature: Falcon-specific EQ firmware reports different band configuration than the app UI expects, causing save/restore mismatch.

title: [Falcon] Custom EQ Save Button — Save EQ with all sliders at 0dB (flat)
steps: Precondition: Falcon device connected. Current EQ is set to a non-flat preset (e.g., 'Bass Boost').
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom
Navigate away: Device → Device Info screen
Navigate back: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
Verify preset name shows 'Custom' (not 'Flat' or 'Bass Boost')
Verify all 6 slider positions are at 0dB
expected: Expected: Preset shows 'Custom' with all sliders at 0dB. Actual (If Bug): Preset reverts to 'Flat' or 'Bass Boost' because the app treats all-zero sliders as 'no change' and skips the save, or the firmware rejects a flat Custom EQ as invalid. Bug signature: Save EQ handler has a guard clause that skips persistence when all values are default (0dB), incorrectly assuming no changes were made.
